title: eriophyllum confertiflorum var. confertiflorum
intro: Eriophyllum confertiflorum var. confertiflorum, also known as Woolly Sunflower, is a perennial shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin irritations, and infections. The health benefits of Eriophyllum confertiflorum var. confertiflorum include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and protecting against microbial infections, as well as supporting immune function and skin health. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
